title: Hank Aaron breaks Babe Ruth's home run record
description: Aaron hit career HR #715 off Al Downing in Atlanta. He finished with 755.

title: President Taft throws first ceremonial first pitch
description: William Howard Taft threw from the stands on Opening Day in Washington, starting a presidential tradition.

title: Jackie Robinson debuts with the Dodgers
description: Robinson breaks the MLB color barrier at Ebbets Field against the Boston Braves.

title: Yankee Stadium opens
description: The original 'House that Ruth Built' hosted its first game. Ruth hit a three-run home run.

title: Ebbets Field opens in Brooklyn
description: The Dodgers' home park opened for its first NL game against the Philadelphia Phillies.

title: Ted Williams hits his first MLB home run
description: The Red Sox rookie connected off Yankees pitcher Bump Hadley.

title: Lou Gehrig ends his consecutive-games streak
description: Gehrig voluntarily benched himself after 2,130 consecutive games, a record that stood until 1995.

title: First televised MLB game
description: NBC broadcast a Columbia-Princeton college game, then a Brooklyn-Cincinnati MLB game on August 26, 1939.

title: First MLB night game
description: Cincinnati hosted the Philadelphia Phillies at Crosley Field under newly installed lights.

title: Baseball Hall of Fame dedication
description: The Hall of Fame opened in Cooperstown, NY with the first inductees: Cobb, Ruth, Wagner, Mathewson, Johnson.

title: Johnny Vander Meer's second consecutive no-hitter
description: Cincinnati's Vander Meer no-hit the Dodgers four days after no-hitting the Braves — still the only back-to-back no-hitters in MLB history.

title: Lou Gehrig's 'Luckiest Man' speech
description: Gehrig, diagnosed with ALS, delivered his farewell speech at Yankee Stadium: 'Today, I consider myself the luckiest man on the face of the earth.'

title: Larry Doby integrates the American League
description: Doby pinch-hit for Cleveland against the White Sox, 11 weeks after Jackie Robinson's NL debut.

title: Babe Ruth's 700th career home run
description: Ruth hit #700 off Detroit's Tommy Bridges at Navin Field, becoming the first player to reach 700.

title: Joe DiMaggio's 56-game hitting streak ends
description: DiMaggio was held hitless by Cleveland on July 17; his streak remains MLB's longest.

title: First radio broadcast of an MLB game
description: KDKA Pittsburgh broadcast the Phillies-Pirates game — the first professional baseball game on the radio.

title: Cal Ripken Jr. breaks Gehrig's consecutive games record
description: Ripken played his 2,131st straight game at Camden Yards — a standing ovation stopped play for 22 minutes.

title: Mark McGwire breaks Roger Maris's home run record
description: McGwire's 62nd home run off the Cubs' Steve Trachtenberg passed the 37-year single-season record.

title: Ted Williams hits .406
description: Williams went 6-for-8 in a season-ending doubleheader to finish at .406 — the last .400 season in MLB history.

title: Roger Maris hits 61st home run
description: Maris broke Babe Ruth's single-season home run record on the final day of the season at Yankee Stadium.

title: Bob Gibson sets World Series strikeout record
description: The Cardinals' Gibson struck out 17 Tigers in Game 1 of the 1968 World Series — still a single-game WS record.

title: Don Larsen's perfect game in the World Series
description: The Yankees' Larsen retired all 27 Brooklyn Dodgers in Game 5 — still the only perfect game in World Series history.

title: Bill Mazeroski walk-off to win the World Series
description: Mazeroski's ninth-inning home run off Ralph Terry gave the Pirates the title over the Yankees — the only walk-off in a Game 7.

title: Kirk Gibson's pinch-hit walk-off
description: Injured Dodgers slugger Gibson limped to the plate in Game 1 of the World Series and hit a two-run walk-off off Dennis Eckersley.

title: Carlton Fisk's Game 6 home run
description: Fisk waved his 12th-inning home run fair at Fenway Park, forcing Game 7 of the 1975 World Series.

title: Red Sox end the Curse of the Bambino
description: Boston swept the Cardinals for its first World Series title since 1918.

title: Cubs end 108-year championship drought
description: Chicago beat Cleveland in Game 7 of the World Series after a 17-minute rain delay before the 10th inning.

title: Christy Mathewson born
description: One of the original five Hall of Fame inductees and a WWI veteran.

title: Pearl Harbor attack reshapes MLB
description: By war's end, more than 500 MLB players had served in the military.

title: Roberto Clemente killed in plane crash
description: Clemente died en route to deliver earthquake aid to Nicaragua. Inducted into Hall of Fame with waived waiting period in 1973.

title: Babe Ruth sold to the Yankees
description: The Red Sox sold Ruth to New York for $100,000 plus a loan — beginning the Curse of the Bambino.

title: National League founded
description: The NL was established in New York City — still in operation, making it the oldest major professional sports league in North America.

title: American League begins play
description: The AL opened as a major league with games in Chicago, Cleveland, Detroit, and Baltimore.

title: Babe Ruth hits his first MLB home run
description: Ruth, then a Red Sox pitcher, hit a solo shot off the Yankees' Jack Warhop.

title: The Galarraga near-perfect game
description: Detroit's Armando Galarraga lost a perfect game on a missed call by umpire Jim Joyce, who apologized publicly the next day.

title: Roger Maris's home-run chase begins in earnest
description: Maris hit his 21st HR of the season; he would pass Babe Ruth on October 1.

title: Ed Delahanty dies at Niagara Falls
description: The HOF outfielder, hitting .333 at the time, died mysteriously after being put off a train near the falls.

title: Ray Chapman killed by a pitch
description: Cleveland shortstop Ray Chapman was struck in the head by a Carl Mays pitch and died the next morning — the only on-field player fatality in MLB history.

title: Eddie Gaedel's one at-bat
description: Bill Veeck's St. Louis Browns sent 3'7" Eddie Gaedel to pinch-hit. He walked on four pitches and never batted again.

title: Billie Jean King beats Bobby Riggs
description: Not MLB — but a defining moment for women in sports that happened during baseball's stretch run.

title: Black Sox scandal regular season ends
description: The White Sox won the AL pennant. Eight players would later be banned for life after the World Series fix.

title: Bobby Thomson's 'Shot Heard 'Round the World'
description: Giants outfielder hit a 3-run HR off Dodgers' Ralph Branca to win the NL pennant playoff.

title: Emmett Ashford becomes first Black umpire in MLB
description: Ashford worked the plate for a Cleveland-Washington game at age 51.

title: First MLB umpire hired
description: William McLean became the first professional umpire in the National League.

title: Minnesota Twins turn unassisted triple play
description: Twins shortstop Billy Ripken — wait, Cleveland's Carlos Baerga — executed rare unassisted triples in the era.

title: Harvey Haddix's 12 perfect innings
description: Pittsburgh's Haddix retired 36 straight Milwaukee Braves before losing in the 13th — the longest perfect game streak in MLB history, though not officially a perfect game.

title: Don Drysdale scoreless innings streak peaks
description: Drysdale completed 58 2/3 consecutive scoreless innings — a record later broken by Orel Hershiser in 1988.

title: John 'Blue Moon' Odom / Francisco Barrios combined no-hitter
description: Two White Sox pitchers combined to no-hit Oakland — the first combined no-hitter in the AL.

title: Willie Mays's over-the-shoulder catch
description: Mays made 'The Catch' in Game 1 of the World Series off Vic Wertz — perhaps the most iconic defensive play in baseball history.

title: Don Mattingly wins AL MVP
description: The Yankees' first baseman hit .324 with 35 HRs and 145 RBIs.

title: Bill Buckner's error in Game 6
description: Mookie Wilson's grounder went through Buckner's legs, keeping the Mets alive and extending the Curse of the Bambino.
